summary:
With an overall score of 92/100, FundedNext ranked 4th in our analysis of the top prop trading firms. As of 2026, it is a broker backed prop firm with one step and two step challenges via the Stellar and Stellar Lite models.

If you are looking to prop trade futures markets rather than CFDs, FundedNext also recently launched a new futures trading branch. With FundedNext Futures, you can now take challenges with real CME futures pricing.
cons: News trading rules on funded accounts
bestFor: Traders who want high profit splits, flexible evaluations, and broker-backed execution
notIdealFor: Traders who need unrestricted news trading or prefer free platform access on all options

categorySections.challenges:
FundedNext offers two main models of prop challenges – **Stellar** and **Stellar Lite**. You can choose between one and two step evaluation processes for the Stellar Challenge, while Stellar Lite is two step only.

**Stellar (One Step)**: A single-phase challenge with 30:1 leverage and a 10% profit target. Best for traders who want a fast track to funding. **Stellar (Two Step)**: Two-phase challenge with 100:1 leverage, 8% then 5% targets. **Stellar Lite (Two Step)**: Lower-cost entry with 100:1 leverage, 8% then 4% targets.

All models offer a **95% profit split** once funded. Stellar 1-Step and 2-Step also give you a **15% profit share during the challenge phase**. The scaling plan allows a **40% account increase** every 4 months, up to **$4 million** max allocation.

FundedNext scored a perfect 10/10 for Challenges thanks to flexible models, strong add-ons, and fair entry costs.
categorySections.spreads:
FundedNext now operates through its own brokerage, **FNmarkets**, launched in May 2025. Spreads and execution are handled directly through FNmarkets' infrastructure rather than third-party brokers.

Commission charges vary by challenge model: **Stellar 1-Step & 2-Step** charge $5 per lot for Forex and Commodities, while **Stellar Lite** charges $7 per lot. Indices and Crypto have **no commission** on any challenge type.

FundedNext scored 9/10 for spreads. While real-time spread data isn't published yet, the broker-backed model should allow tighter spreads and improved execution long term.
categorySections.markets:
FundedNext offers access to **83 financial markets** across four asset classes: **50 forex pairs** (majors, minors, exotics), **18 indices**, **5 commodities** (precious metals and energy), and **10 cryptocurrencies** including Bitcoin and Ethereum.

FundedNext scored 9/10 for markets, providing flexibility for different trading strategies and risk tolerances.
categorySections.platforms:
FundedNext provides access to four trading platforms – **MT4**, **MT5** (with TradingView integration), **cTrader**, and **Match-Trader**. MT4 and MT5 are free, while cTrader and Match-Trader carry a **$25 platform fee** and aren't available on the largest account sizes.

MT4 is suited for EA and indicator-focused traders. MT5 adds advanced order types and a built-in economic calendar. cTrader offers Level II market depth pricing and clean interface. Match-Trader is browser-based with no downloads required.

FundedNext scored 9/10 for platforms thanks to the variety of options covering different trading styles.
categorySections.payouts:
FundedNext supports payments via debit/credit cards (Visa, Mastercard), bank transfer, Perfect Money, and crypto (USDT, BTC, ETH). Payouts are handled through **Deel** with a minimum withdrawal of **$20**.

Profit splits start at **95%** once funded, with Stellar models also offering a **15% share during the challenge**. Payout schedule varies: Stellar 1-Step is every 5 business days, Stellar 2-Step starts monthly then moves to bi-weekly, and Stellar Lite starts at 21 days then moves to bi-weekly.

FundedNext scored 9/10 for payouts thanks to the high profit splits, flexible scheduling, and reliable processing through Deel.
categorySections.support:
FundedNext offers **24/7 customer support** through email and live chat. Funded traders benefit from **Pro Support** connecting them with in-house technical experts.

Educational resources include trading guides, an Economic Calendar, Psychology Feed sessions, and a **YouTube channel** with 77K+ subscribers and 1,200+ educational videos. The Risk Reward Radio podcast offers deeper insights into trader journeys.

FundedNext scored well for support thanks to round-the-clock availability and a strong suite of educational materials.
categorySections.trust:
FundedNext became a **broker-backed prop firm** with the launch of **FNmarkets** in 2025. This gives them control over execution, spreads, and trading infrastructure.

TrustPilot score is **4.5/5** with an 'Excellent' ranking. The community spans **226,800+ Discord** members, **254,000 Instagram** followers, **167,000 Twitter** followers, **135,000 Facebook** followers, and a **59,400+ Telegram** channel.

FundedNext scored 9/10 for trust thanks to the broker-backed model, excellent TrustPilot rating, and one of the largest prop trading communities online.

verdictBlocks.heading: Our Final Verdict On FundedNext
verdictBlocks.body:
FundedNext earns a 92/100 and currently ranks 4th on our list of top prop trading firms. With both one-step and two-step evaluations, up to 95% profit splits, and strong platform support, it ticks the right boxes for traders looking for flexibility and fast payouts. The Stellar Lite model is especially cost-effective for beginners, while the add-ons make it easy to tailor each challenge to your style and goals.

The big update last year was FundedNext's shift to a broker-backed model with the launch of FNmarkets. This gives them more control over execution, pricing, and payments — something few prop firms can offer. Combined with a solid Trustpilot rating, large trading community, and strong payout record, FundedNext continues to be a reliable option whether you're new to prop trading or scaling up.

faq.a: Yes, FundedNext is a legitimate prop trading firm with account sizes from $5,000 to $200,000 and profit splits up to 95%. In 2025, it became a broker-backed firm through the launch of FNmarkets, giving it greater control over execution and trading conditions, and is now considered one of the best prop trading firms available in 2026.

Yes, prop firms do pay traders who meet their profit targets and follow the firm's rules. Payments are based on profit splits, typically ranging from 70% to 95%. Reputable firms like FundedNext and FTMO have a track record of reliable payouts, often processed through secure third-party platforms like Deel. However, it's important to choose a trustworthy prop firm to avoid scams.

FundedNext works by offering traders a chance to manage funded accounts after passing one of its two challenge models: Stellar or Stellar Lite. Traders must meet specific profit targets while adhering to daily and overall loss limits. After passing the challenge, traders can earn profit splits up to 95% on live accounts.

faq.q: What are the profit split percentages for FundedNext traders?
faq.a: FundedNext offers profit splits of up to 95%. Traders on Stellar 1-Step and 2-Step accounts receive a 15% profit share during the challenge and 95% once funded. Stellar Lite accounts don't pay during the challenge but also unlock 95% profit splits after funding.
